steps.title: Decode the function notation
steps.content: From the problem, the function describes a beam where $x$ is the depth in millimeters below the surface and $f(x)$ is the predicted intensity in photons.

steps.title: Translate f(11) = 300 to English
steps.content:
Verify by evaluating the function at $x = 11$:
$$f(11) = 600(0.5)^{\frac{11}{11}} = 600(0.5) = 300$$
So when $x = 11$ mm (depth) and $f(11) = 300$ photons (intensity), the statement means: a beam 11 millimeters below the surface has a predicted intensity of 300 photons. That matches choice C — C is correct.
